notes: Tested SKGLView with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 and 3.119.2 on iOS simulator (iPhone 16 Pro, iOS 26.2) with .NET 10.0.102. Both versions rendered correctly — CornflowerBlue background with white text, no EAGL errors. The iOS simulator translates OpenGL ES calls to Metal via com.apple.metal.simulator, which masks the real-device EAGL binding failure. The reporter's error 'Failed to bind EAGLDrawable' is specific to CAEAGLLayer on physical iOS devices where native EAGL/OpenGL ES is used directly. A real iOS device is needed to reproduce this bug.

reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new maui -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Controls --version 3.116.0

filesCreated.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:skia="clr-namespace:SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Controls;assembly=SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Controls"
             x:Class="Repro.MainPage">
    <Grid RowDefinitions="Auto,*">
        <Label Grid.Row="0" Text="SKGLView EAGL Test (Issue #3428)" FontSize="20" HorizontalOptions="Center" Padding="10" />
        <skia:SKGLView Grid.Row="1" x:Name="GlView" PaintSurface="OnGLPaintSurface" />
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>

reproductionSteps.description: Install and launch app on iPhone 16 Pro simulator (iOS 26.2)
reproductionSteps.command: xcrun simctl install <sim-id> Repro.app && xcrun simctl launch <sim-id> com.companyname.repro
reproductionSteps.output: App launched successfully. Log stream shows: 'OnGLPaintSurface called - GRContext null: False' (called twice). No EAGL errors, no OpenGL errors, no framebuffer errors.

reproductionSteps.description: Verify rendering output via simulator screenshot
reproductionSteps.output: Screenshot captured. SKGLView renders CornflowerBlue background with white 'Hello SkiaSharp GL!' text. No white canvas observed. The simulator log shows com.apple.metal.simulator connections — confirming EAGL calls are translated to Metal on the simulator.

reproductionSteps.description: Test with latest stable SkiaSharp 3.119.2 in fresh project on same simulator
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3428-latest && dotnet new maui && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Controls --version 3.119.2 && dotnet build -f net10.0-ios -r iossimulator-arm64 && xcrun simctl install && xcrun simctl launch
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded. App renders identically to 3.116.0 — CornflowerBlue background, white text visible. OnGLPaintSurface called with non-null GRContext. No EAGL errors.

versionResults.notes: SKGLView renders correctly on iOS simulator. OnGLPaintSurface fires, GRContext is non-null, CornflowerBlue background with text visible. No EAGL binding errors. Simulator translates OpenGL ES to Metal, masking potential real-device EAGL failures.
Same behavior as 3.116.0 — renders correctly on iOS simulator with no EAGL errors.

actionability.reason: Bug could not be reproduced on iOS simulator because the simulator translates OpenGL ES to Metal, masking the EAGL binding failure. The reporter's 'Failed to bind EAGLDrawable' error is specific to real iOS devices. Need confirmation of device model, iOS version, and a minimal reproduction project to test on physical hardware.

actions.description: Post reproduction attempt results with request for device details

proposedResponse.summary: Not reproduced on iOS simulator; requesting real device details
proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the report. We attempted to reproduce this on an iOS simulator (iPhone 16 Pro, iOS 26.2) with .NET 10.0.102 using both SkiaSharp 3.116.0 and 3.119.2.

On the simulator, SKGLView renders correctly — the `PaintSurface` event fires, `GRContext` is non-null, and the canvas displays the expected content (not white). No "Failed to bind EAGLDrawable" errors appear in the logs.

This is likely because the iOS simulator translates OpenGL ES (EAGL) calls through Apple's Metal translation layer (`com.apple.metal.simulator`), which masks the real-device EAGL binding failure.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Simulator Result |
|---------|------------------|
| 3.116.0 | ✅ Renders correctly |
| 3.119.2 | ✅ Renders correctly |

To help us reproduce on a real device, could you share:
1. The specific iOS device model and iOS version
2. A minimal reproduction project (or confirm if a simple SKGLView with `PaintSurface` is enough to trigger it)
3. Whether switching to `SKCanvasView` (non-GL, CPU rendering) works as a workaround

The underlying issue may be that the iOS `SKGLView` uses deprecated `EAGLContext` (OpenGL ES), which Apple deprecated in iOS 12. The MacCatalyst handler already uses Metal (`SKMetalView`) — the iOS handler may need the same migration.

missingInfo: Specific iOS device model and iOS version
Whether SKCanvasView (non-GL) works as a workaround
Whether the issue occurs on all .NET 10 iOS devices or specific hardware only

corrections.upstream: Triage assumed bug would reproduce on any iOS .NET 10 environment
corrections.corrected: Bug does not reproduce on iOS simulator — simulator translates EAGL to Metal. Real device required.
